summary: Google's FlightsFrontendService RPC rejects non-interactive clients (ErrorResponse code 13 in the wrb.fr envelope); a blocked envelope must never read as a legitimate empty result, and flights/dates must fall back to the server-rendered tfs= search page, whose embedded AF_initDataCallback payload uses the same inner schema as the RPC.
reason: Around 2026-06-09 Google gated GetShoppingResults and GetCalendarGraph for every non-browser client (verified against fli 0.10.0 with curl-cffi impersonation and against Google's own UI in headless Chromium) — 'flights' silently returned success with 0 results and 'dates' hard-errored. The gate is server-side; no request-shape tweak fixes it. The RPC paths stay primary so an un-gating restores fast behavior automatically; the fallback (one GET per search, per-day fan-out for dates) is the only data path that works while gated.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/gflights/... passes (blocked-envelope detection + HTML extraction against live 2026-06-11 captures, old-format fixtures still parse); live dogfood: 'flights AUS LAX 2026-07-15' returns 13 itineraries via fallback (was 0), 'dates AUS LAX --from 2026-07-13 --to 2026-07-19' prices all 7 days (was hard error).
